title: Asia 21 Next Generation Fellows
text:
Meet the Class of 2025

Summit Recap

The Move As One Coalition has won the WRI Global Cities Prize, supported by the WeSolve Foundation under the leadership of Asia 21 Fellow Ken Abante.

Leaders, advisors and volunteers for the Move As One Coalition pose for a photo op with their bikes in Manila. The coalition has turned fragmented demands into collective action, reframing transportation as a public service. Photo: WRI Ross Center

Asia 21 Fellow Bam Aquino Wins Senate Seat

Voices of Asia 21

Current Affairs

New York

Asia 21 @ 20: Connecting Leaders, Driving Change | Martin Tan

A candid conversation with Martin Tan on leadership, friendship, philanthropy, and why Asia 21 alumni network matters more than ever in a fragmented world.

Watch Now

Policy

 / 
New York

South Asia’s Youth Moment: A New Generation Rewriting the Region’s Politics and Shared Future

A panel of experts discuss how South Asia’s youth are reshaping politics, from Gen Z-led movements in Nepal to youth mobilization in Bangladesh and mass protests in Sri Lanka.

Take Note: Artificial Intelligence, Power, and the Public Interest

Latha Reddy, Nikhil Pahwa, and Sunaina Kumar unpack how societies can confront AI's risks while safeguarding democracy, trust, and the public interest.

[Summer Summit: China] Chinese Creativity: Home and Abroad

Huang Rui, Jia Jiawei, Jiang Qiong’er, Capucine Cogné, and Ruohan Xie explore how Chinese artistic expression sparks cross-cultural dialogue across borders.

Unpacking the Thailand-Cambodia Border Conflict

Sophal Ear, Supalak Ganjanakhundee, Benjamin Zawacki, and Bryanna Entwistle discuss the ceasefire, ASEAN’s role, U.S. engagement, and the implications for Thai politics.

The State of Myanmar’s Civil War: Military Dynamics and ASEAN’s Ongoing Dilemma

Derek Mitchell, Thitinan Pongsudhirak, Nyantha Maw Lin, and Meghan Murphy discuss the evolving military situation and assess how ASEAN is influencing the course of the conflict.

New York

Political Reset? Implications of the ROK Election for the Alliance and the Region

The Asia Society Policy Institute hosts a rapid reaction panel discussion on the election of Lee Jae-myung in South Korea's snap presidential election, and the implications for the U.S.-ROK alliance and South Korea's relations.

"Leave No One Behind”: Development Impacts on Vulnerable Groups in Southeast Asia

Prapaporn Tivayanond Mongkhonvanit examines the impact of U.S. and Chinese development programs on marginalized and vulnerable groups in Southeast Asia.
